text: GPT-5.6 found a new 7,194-point Sidon construction. A separate verifier checked all 25,880,415 pair sums. Vela routed the evidence to Defer. Accepted scientific state changed by zero.

That separation is the product. Models can propose work. Frozen verifiers can check artifacts. Vela can record evidence and apply policy. But none of them can make the protected scientific decision. Verification is not acceptance.

For Build Week, Canopus gave GPT-5.6 one exact mission: improve a mechanically verified but unaccepted 7,193-point subset of the 24-dimensional Boolean cube. The registration binds the model, target packet, artifact path, byte and time budgets, a frozen verifier, an expected Defer route, and zero accepted-state change. It explicitly forbids claims of maximality, classification, acceptance, or a world record.

Inside its bounded workspace, GPT-5.6 built the exact conflict structure and found a one-for-two exchange. It removed baseline point 72, added hexadecimal points 970f25 and 246891, reconstructed 7,194 points, and independently enumerated every pair sum before handing off.

Then a separate, network-denied, read-only verifier parsed the frozen 359-kilobyte artifact. It checked 7,194 distinct binary points and generated all 25,880,415 unordered componentwise sums. Zero collisions. Exact claim binding. Exit zero. The worker never graded itself.

Canopus passed the verified artifact to Vela. Vela built a root-bound Receipt and signed policy routed the proposal to Defer. Clean-clone replay matched. The public Observatory shows the whole chain: mission, GPT-5.6 worker, artifact, verifier, Receipt, and Defer. The candidate lower bound advances from 7,193 to 7,194, while the accepted state and the human decision remain untouched.

The boundary is real, not decorative. In a separate formal mission, GPT-5.6 produced a Lean candidate with an unsolved inverse goal and a dependency on sorry Ax. The frozen verifier returned exit one. Canopus produced no Receipt, no proposal, no frontier commit, and no mutation. Failed work stays failed.
Canopus 0.6.2 is public on npm with SLSA provenance, composed against Vela 0.912.0, with the read-only Observatory as its evidence surface.
Judges can inspect the package in ninety seconds, or reproduce the exact public frontier commit without rebuilding Canopus.

This Codex task built Canopus, bounded the GPT-5.6 mission, repaired two fail-closed dogfooding defects, shipped the release, and published the governed evidence flow. Give Codex a mission. Verify the work. Keep humans in authority. Let agents do the work without letting them declare truth.
